skill.name: screenfull-fullscreen-api
skill.description: Guides Angular developers in adding cross-browser fullscreen features with the screenfull library.
skill.summary: Build Angular fullscreen toggles, viewers, dashboards, and presentation modes with screenfull.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: The static scan reported many high and critical patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, fullscreen API names, keyboard examples, and documentation URLs. No prompt injection, credential access, obfuscation, or malicious exfiltration evidence was found in SKILL.md. The main confirmed concern is an Angular [innerHTML] example that could encourage unsafe rendering if adapted to user-controlled content.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe HTML Rendering Example
medium_findings.description: The presentation mode example binds slide content with Angular [innerHTML]. The provided slide content is static, so this is not malicious, but the skill does not warn users to sanitize or avoid user-controlled HTML. A copied implementation could create cross-site scripting risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The [innerHTML] binding is directly visible in the Angular template. The sample data is static, which lowers confidence that this is an exploitable issue in the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Documented Package Installation Requires Network Access
low_findings.description: The skill includes a yarn add command for screenfull and links to external documentation. This is visible documentation rather than hidden network behavior, but users should understand that following these instructions contacts package and documentation hosts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network-relevant content is limited to a package installation command and explicit reference links. I found no hidden HTTP client code or data transmission logic.

low_findings.title: Static Code Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings align with Markdown fenced code blocks and TypeScript examples. The skill contains instructional snippets, not executable marketplace scripts, so these findings do not indicate command execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review confirms the reported backticks are Markdown code fences. No skill-owned script file or runtime command execution path exists in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Other High-Severity Static Matches Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, certificate or key file, system reconnaissance, and critical combination alerts map to ordinary fullscreen, keyboard, DOM, and API reference examples. No evidence found of cryptographic routines, certificate handling, credential access, reconnaissance, or obfuscation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas contain Angular imports, keyboard listener examples, and event.key handling. These contexts do not support the scanner labels for crypto, keys, or malicious chaining.

dangerous_patterns.title: Angular innerHTML Binding
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates rendering slide content through [innerHTML]. This pattern is safe only for trusted or sanitized HTML and should not be copied for user-controlled content without explicit protection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in the documented example. The risk depends on how future users source slide content.

content.value_statement: Fullscreen behavior is inconsistent across browsers and easy to wire incorrectly. This skill provides Angular examples using screenfull for toggles, viewers, dashboards, and presentation modes.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install screenfull 6.x with yarn.
Provides Angular examples for document and element fullscreen toggles.
Demonstrates a fullscreen image gallery viewer with a close action.
Outlines presentation mode with slide navigation and keyboard handling.
Lists screenfull methods, integration checks, browser support, and references.
limitations: Focused on Angular and the ng-events project style.
Does not include automated tests or a complete application integration.
Includes an [innerHTML] example without sanitization guidance.
Mobile browser fullscreen support can be limited, especially on iOS Safari.

use_cases.title: Add dashboard fullscreen mode
use_cases.description: Build an Angular dashboard control that expands a specific container and updates state when fullscreen changes.

use_cases.title: Create an image review viewer
use_cases.description: Use the gallery pattern to let users inspect construction photos in a focused fullscreen view.

use_cases.title: Review fullscreen implementation quality
use_cases.description: Check browser support handling, exit controls, event listener cleanup, and mobile limitations before release.

prompt_templates.title: Add a basic toggle
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a fullscreen toggle to my Angular component using screenfull. Check browser support and show clear enter and exit states.

prompt_templates.title: Fullscreen one element
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Angular fullscreen control for one dashboard container. Use screenfull, track state, and include styles for fullscreen mode.

prompt_templates.title: Build a media viewer
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a fullscreen image viewer for my Angular gallery. Include open, close, state reset, browser fallback, and accessible controls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit fullscreen safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Angular fullscreen implementation for unsafe HTML rendering, event listener cleanup, browser support, spoofing risk, and mobile behavior.

output_examples.input: Request: Add fullscreen support to a project dashboard.
output_examples.output: A plan for a dashboard container reference, a screenfull toggle, state updates on fullscreen changes, and fullscreen-specific styling.

output_examples.input: Request: Create a presentation mode for project slides.
output_examples.output: A component design with present and exit actions, slide navigation, keyboard handling, and warnings about trusted slide content.

output_examples.input: Request: Check whether my fullscreen code is ready for release.
output: Test Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and mobile behavior.

best_practices: Check screenfull.isEnabled before every fullscreen action.
Provide a visible exit control and handle the Escape key gracefully.
Sanitize or avoid HTML content before rendering it inside fullscreen views.
anti_patterns: Do not force fullscreen without a user action.
Do not use fullscreen for sensitive or deceptive workflows.
Do not bind user-controlled content through [innerHTML] without protection.

faq.question: What library does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the screenfull library as a wrapper around the browser Fullscreen API.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to Angular?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use Angular component patterns and ng-zorro style icons.

faq.question: Does it support fullscreen for a single element?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows toggling fullscreen for a specific dashboard container or viewer element.

faq.question: Does the skill include browser compatibility guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists common browser support notes and warns about limited iOS Safari behavior.

faq.question: What security issue should users watch for?
faq.answer: Avoid rendering user-controlled HTML with [innerHTML] unless it is sanitized or trusted.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning about the unsafe HTML rendering pattern in one example.
